title: Fix PR 144 review Beads batch
description: Address the PR #144 review findings filed as Beads: tax snapshot status race, WooCommerce tax fallback under-taxing, pending FX queue dedupe, mutable purchase tax statuses, tax snapshot N+1 updates, and cost-layer revaluation audit storage overhead.
close_reason: Superseded by the exact PR #144 review Beads imported from codex/dead-stock-report and closed individually: xjj, xin, rdy, dmi, jox, lvs.

title: WC tax fallback block-policy allows fallback to zero-rate even when destination should be taxed (PR #144)
acceptance_criteria:
- Add a second blocking condition: when the destination country has any active non-zero tax rate configured for the product category and the resolver fell back to a different (zero or otherwise non-matching) rate, block the import
- Alternatively: require that the resolver's matched rate ID actually matches the destination jurisdiction (countryCode == destCountry) — block on any fallback regardless of rate value
- Add a focused WC import test for the zero-rated fallback case where the destination should have been non-zero
- Update the Sales Settings fallback widget to also surface zero-rate fallback events
notes: Identified during PR #144 review. The PR addressed bd onetwo3d-ims-xx4 in the over-rated direction but the under-rated direction (silent zero-rate import for a non-zero-rated jurisdiction) is the more dangerous failure mode because under-reported VAT is harder to detect than over-reported VAT.
close_reason: Already fixed on development: WooCommerce tax fallback now compares against expected destination tax rate and blocks zero-rate fallback for taxable destinations; verified by tests/connectors/woocommerce-tax-fallback.test.ts.

title: Tax snapshot refresh has a read-then-write race against status transitions (PR #144)
description:
PR #144 adds `refreshMutableDocumentTaxSnapshotsForRate()` (lib/tax/document-tax-snapshot-refresh.ts) which is called transactionally from `updateTaxRate()` when a TaxRate is edited. The function:

1. `findMany` for SalesOrderLines whose `order.status IN [DRAFT, PENDING_PAYMENT]`.
2. Loops through each line and calls `salesOrderLine.update()` plus header `salesOrder.update()` to apply the new rate.

The findMany result is materialized at read time. If a SalesOrder transitions from DRAFT → PROCESSING (or PENDING_PAYMENT → PROCESSING) between the findMany and the update, the function silently rewrites tax fields on an order that should now be immutable.

The transaction surrounding it doesn't take a SELECT FOR UPDATE on the orders, so concurrent writers can sneak in.

Same gap exists in `refreshMutablePurchaseOrderTaxSnapshots` (status set `[DRAFT, RFQ_SENT, QUOTE_RECEIVED]`).

**Why this matters:** Tax rate edits typically happen during operator setup, but a user shipping an order at the same moment as another user editing the tax rate can cause the shipped order's persisted tax to silently change. Hard to detect post-hoc.
acceptance_criteria:
- Either (a) add SELECT FOR UPDATE on the SalesOrder/PurchaseOrder rows before re-checking status and applying updates, or (b) re-check the order status inside the update by using a conditional update (`update where: { id, status: { in: MUTABLE_STATUSES } }`) and counting affected rows
- Add a concurrency test that exercises the race: start the tax refresh transaction, transition the order to PROCESSING from another connection, assert no update lands on the now-immutable order
- Document the chosen safety mechanism in lib/tax/document-tax-snapshot-refresh.ts
notes: Identified during PR #144 review. The risk is bounded by how rarely operators edit a TaxRate concurrently with order processing, but the failure mode is silent — tax fields are denormalized onto orders/lines, so a stale-write here changes accounting numbers without an audit trail beyond the tax_rate_snapshot_refresh activity log entry (which doesn't include which lines were touched).
close_reason: Already fixed on development: tax snapshot refresh locks parent documents with FOR UPDATE and re-checks mutable status before line/header updates; verified by tests/tax-document-snapshot-refresh.test.ts.

title: Add action-level idempotency test: cancelPurchaseOrder twice (P4.4 follow-up)
description:
Plan item P4.4 closed via PR #138, but only the `isPurchaseOrderCancellationNoop` helper has a test. `tests/domain/purchasing/po-cancellation.test.ts:246` asserts the boolean returns true/false for given statuses. The plan acceptance criterion specifies an action-level test:

> Call `cancelPurchaseOrder` twice; assert second call succeeds silently.

No such test exists. The helper test cannot prove that `cancelPurchaseOrder` actually wires the noop check, that the second call:
- returns `{ success: true }` (not an error)
- does NOT write a second 'cancelled' activity log row
- does NOT enqueue a second accounting sync
- does NOT re-emit stock-sync events

**Why this matters:** A future refactor that removes the noop check (or moves it after the side-effects) would not be caught by the current helper test. Repeated cancellation requests producing duplicate Xero journals or activity logs is the failure mode P4.4 was meant to prevent.
acceptance_criteria:
- Add test in tests/domain/purchasing/po-cancellation.test.ts (or action-level test file) that:
  - Sets up a CANCELLED PO
  - Calls `cancelPurchaseOrder('po-1')` (or fixtures the action call)
  - Asserts result is `{ success: true }`
  - Asserts no duplicate activity log row added
  - Asserts no duplicate accounting sync queue row added
  - Asserts no duplicate stock-sync enqueue
- Update docs/plan.md P4.4 Tests pointer to name the new test
notes: Identified during PR #140 review audit. Same gap pattern as P1.3 (helper test only; action-level integration not proven). My PR #138 review inline finding #6 flagged this explicitly with suggested test shape — recommend implementing that shape.
close_reason: Fixed: tests/domain/purchasing/po-cancellation.test.ts now calls cancelPurchaseOrder twice through an in-memory action fixture and asserts the replay returns success without a duplicate cancellation log, accounting sync, stock-sync enqueue, transaction, or reversal. docs/plan.md P4.4 test pointer updated. Verified by npx tsx --test tests/domain/purchasing/po-cancellation.test.ts.

title: Add action-level idempotency test: createSalesOrderRefund replay (P1.3 follow-up)
description:
Plan item P1.3 closed via PR #128, but only the `applyReturnInboundStockTx` helper has a test for the idempotency-conflict path. The plan acceptance criterion specifies an action-level replay test:

> Call `processRefund` twice with same key, assert one set of movements and one set of layers.

No such test exists. `tests/domain/sales/refund-service.test.ts` does not call `createSalesOrderRefund` twice with the same idempotency key to verify the action-level idempotency.

**Why this matters:** A future refactor that strips the helper-level guard (or wires it incorrectly into the action) would not be caught by current tests. Idempotency regressions in refund flows produce duplicate stock movements and double-posted COGS reversals — exactly the failure mode P1.3 was meant to prevent.
acceptance_criteria:
- Add test in tests/domain/sales/refund-service.test.ts that:
  - Calls `createSalesOrderRefund` once with a refund payload + external refund ID
  - Calls it again with the same (orderId, refundLineId, warehouseId, externalRefundId)
  - Asserts state.movements length is unchanged after the second call
  - Asserts state.costLayers length is unchanged
  - Asserts the second call returns success (not duplicate error)
- Update docs/plan.md P1.3 Tests pointer to name the new test
notes: Identified during PR #140 review audit. Plan currently points to non-existent path `tests/refund-service.idempotency.test.ts`. Helper-level tests at refund-service.test.ts:1150 (applyReturnInboundStockTx idempotency conflict) and woocommerce-refund-sync.test.ts:115 (syncWcRefund repeated delivery) cover adjacent paths but not the action-level replay the plan asked for.
close_reason: Fixed: createSalesOrderRefund now returns an idempotent success for an existing external refund id before validation or side effects, and tests/domain/sales/refund-service.test.ts covers replay without duplicate movements, cost layers, refund rows, refund lines, or stock quantity changes. docs/plan.md P1.3 test pointer updated. Verified by npx tsx --test tests/domain/sales/refund-service.test.ts.

title: Accounting invariant report: exclude REFUNDED/CANCELLED orders
description:
PR #15 (merged) added the accounting invariant report at lib/domain/accounting/invariants.ts but did not exclude terminal-state orders from the scanner.

Without the exclusion:
- CANCELLED orders with prior revenueDeferredDate fire sales_order_revenue_deferral_without_sync_evidence indefinitely (no Group B batch will ever sync against a cancelled order).
- REFUNDED orders with shipmentJournalDate fire shipment_posted_without_sync_evidence permanently.
- The { refunds: { some: {} } } OR-arm pulls in every refunded order, generating false-positive refund-missing findings that desensitize operators.

Fix:
1. Add status: { notIn: ['REFUNDED', 'CANCELLED'] } to salesOrder.findMany where clause (L495-L503).
2. Add order: { status: { notIn: ['REFUNDED', 'CANCELLED'] } } to shipment.findMany (L535-L553).
3. Add a collection test mirroring PR #8's 'inventory row collection excludes fully refunded orders' to lock in the filter.

Reference: https://github.com/OneTwo3D/IMS/pull/15#pullrequestreview-4177148955
close_reason: Already fixed on development: accounting invariant collection excludes REFUNDED/CANCELLED sales orders and shipments whose orders are terminal; verified by tests/domain/accounting/invariants.test.ts.

title: [bug] PO FX rate is manual operator input — no auto-lookup or validation
description:
Unlike SO creation (which calls resolveFxRateToBase to look up the latest rate ≤ order date) and WC order import (which calls getFxRateToGbp), PO creation at app/actions/purchase-orders.ts:787 expects the caller to supply fxRateToBase from the UI. The operator types it in.

Impact: typo, missing decimal, or stale rate from an old browser tab corrupts the unit cost stamped onto every CostLayer created from that PO's receipt — and that base cost is the one consumed by FIFO into customer COGS. Hard to detect after the fact.

Fix direction:
- Default the field from resolveFxRateToBase() at the PO's order date. Allow override but show a delta vs current rate and warn if >2%.
- Validate input is within a sane band of the latest known rate before saving.

Refs:
- app/actions/purchase-orders.ts:787
- app/actions/sales.ts:615 resolveFxRateToBase() (the pattern to follow)
close_reason: Fixed: purchase-order create/edit paths now resolve the latest stored FX rate for the PO currency at save time and reject manual overrides outside a 2% tolerance; reorder-forecast draft POs also stamp the resolved rate instead of 1. Verified by npx tsx --test tests/domain/purchasing/purchase-order-fx.test.ts and npm run type-check.

title: [bug] Tax-rate fallback to order default is silent on WC import and resolver
description:
When an incoming WooCommerce order's externalTaxRateId has no ShoppingTaxRateMapping, importer falls back to resolveLineTaxRateBatch(); when the resolver's 4-step lookup (country+category → country+STANDARD → global+category) finds nothing, it falls back to the order-default rate with only a console warning. Same silent fallback exists in createSalesOrder/createPurchaseOrder.

Impact: a misconfigured tax mapping (or a new country added to WC without IMS setup) will quietly produce sales at the wrong VAT rate. The discrepancy only surfaces when Xero rejects the TaxType or on a manual VAT review.

Fix direction:
1. Block import (or require explicit override) when fallback is used for a non-zero-rated jurisdiction.
2. Record fallback events to activity log per line so operations can audit.
3. Add a settings-page widget listing recent fallback events.

Refs:
- lib/tax/resolve-rate.ts:53 pickTaxRate()
- lib/connectors/woocommerce/sync/order-import.ts:112
close_reason: Already fixed on development: WooCommerce fallback imports block unsafe fallback rates, sales/purchase creation logs tax_rate_fallback events, blocked imports log tax_rate_fallback_blocked, and settings exposes recent fallback activity.

title: [bug] Retroactive cost-layer snapshot patching has no audit trail
description:
When a PO's landed cost finalises after shipments have already shipped, `updateSnapshotsForCostLayerChange()` (lib/cost-layers.ts:291) silently rewrites the JSON `costLayerSnapshot` on every affected ShipmentLine, OrderAllocation, SalesOrderRefundLine and StockTransferLine, then refreshes denormalised `Shipment.cogsBatchAmount` and `SalesOrderLine.cogsBase`.

No row is written to the activity log; nothing records previous vs new unit cost. P&L and margin reports change retroactively with no breadcrumb.

Fix direction: write a typed activity-log entry per affected line with old/new `unitCostBase` and the triggering CostLayer id. Consider keeping a CostLayerAdjustment table for full lineage.

Refs:
- lib/cost-layers.ts:291 updateSnapshotsForCostLayerChange()
- See onetwo3d-ims-a8u for the originating reconciliation work
close_reason: Already fixed on development: updateSnapshotsForCostLayerChange writes cost_layer_snapshot_revalued activity-log rows with previous/new unit cost metadata for each patched snapshot row, and landed-cost recalculation records transaction-critical LandedCostRevaluationRun audit rows. Verified by npx tsx --test tests/domain/purchasing/landed-cost-service.test.ts tests/cost-layers.test.ts.

title: Manual Mark Received on a partially-booked transfer double-counts stock
description:
Codex P1 from review of branch vs main.

When a Mintsoft (WMS) callback partially books in a transfer:
- lib/connectors/mintsoft/sync/booked-in-handler.ts increments transferLine.qtyReceived
- Posts a TRANSFER_IN movement of the partial qty
- Creates cost layers from a slice of the snapshot proportional to that qty
- Leaves transfer.status = IN_TRANSIT until qtyReceived >= qty for every line

The manual close-out path in app/actions/transfers.ts (receiveTransfer) was still:
- Posting a TRANSFER_IN of the FULL Number(line.qty)
- Recreating cost layers from the entire snapshot
- Setting qtyReceived: qty

Net effect: destination stock and cost layers overstated by the already-booked amount whenever an operator closed out a partial WMS transfer through the manual button.

Fix: receive only the remaining qty (qty - qtyReceived), slice the snapshot via sliceTransferSnapshotForReceipt to grab only the un-consumed cost layer portion, skip lines that are already fully received via WMS.
close_reason: Closed

title: Cost-layer deletion leaves ghost snapshot entries
description:
updateSnapshotsForCostLayerChange silently skips deleted layers. ShipmentLine / OrderAllocation / SalesOrderRefundLine / StockTransferLine snapshots can keep stale unitCostBase entries pointing at layers that no longer exist. No FK constraint prevents the deletion.

Refs:
- lib/cost-layer-snapshots.ts (parser/reducer)
- lib/cost-layers.ts:291-344 (updateSnapshotsForCostLayerChange)

Fix direction: add FK from snapshot JSON references → CostLayer (or denormalise to a join table), and either block deletion if referenced or cascade-update.
close_reason: Verified: not a bug. Cost layer deletion in stock.ts:710 is gated by line 703-708: throws if consumedQty > 0 (any prior FIFO consumption blocks the delete and forces user to create a reversing adjustment). An unconsumed layer has no shipment/allocation/refund snapshot referring to it, so 'ghost snapshots' cannot occur. The other delete site (reset.ts:63) is the admin DB-wipe path — out of scope. No FK constraint needed because the gate already prevents the failure mode.

title: Manufacturing disassembly over-attributes cost-layer source qty
description:
componentShare is multiplied into source line qty per component. Sum of component source-line quantities exceeds the original recovered layer qty, breaking traceability for downstream landed-cost recalculation.

Refs:
- app/actions/manufacturing.ts:824-831
close_reason: Verified: not a bug. Source-line qty across components sums correctly. componentShare = baseAllocatedCost / totalRecoveredCostBase, and Σ baseAllocatedCost = totalRecoveredCostBase by construction (buildDisassemblyRecoveryPlan: Σ historicalCost + residualCostBase redistribution = Σ entryCostBase). So Σ (entry.qty × componentShare_i) = entry.qty × Σ shares = entry.qty × 1 = original recovered layer qty. The agent's 'sum exceeds' claim is incorrect. There is a minor rounding-drift risk via componentShare.toNumber() (Prisma.Decimal → JS number), but that's a separate concern with negligible impact.

title: Allocation snapshots editable between A2 staging and Group B run
description:
resetAllocationAccountingIfStaged() only triggers when shipmentJournalDate is set — i.e. after Group B has stamped the order. Between A2 staging and Group B, allocations can be edited and the next batch will use stale snapshots. Shipment-line snapshots are also not cleared on reset (only allocation snapshots are).

Refs:
- app/actions/allocation.ts:42, :74-77

Fix direction: lock allocations on A2 staging and require an explicit unstage that clears both allocation- and shipment-line snapshots.
close_reason: Verified: not a bug. resetAllocationAccountingIfStaged (allocation.ts:42-79) is called inside every allocation-mutation tx (e.g. deallocateOrder line 937). When inventoryAllocatedDate is set: (a) blocks edits if any shipment has shipmentJournalDate (Group B already ran) — line 55-64; (b) otherwise clears inventoryAllocatedDate AND OrderAllocation.costLayerSnapshot so A2 rebuilds on next batch. ShipmentLine snapshots are intentionally NOT cleared because they reflect the actual SHIPPED state and should be preserved. The 'stale snapshots in next batch' claim is incorrect.

title: StockLevel.quantity drifts from sum of cost-layer remainingQty
description:
StockLevel.quantity is mutated directly in several places and is not derived from the cost layers. Partial transaction failures or layer adjustments can leave the two out of sync. There is no reconciliation job.

Refs:
- lib/stock.ts:166-172, :193-197 (direct StockLevel mutations)
- lib/transfers.ts:558-559 (transfer mutations)
- lib/cost-layers.ts:181-197 (layer mutations not synced back)

Fix direction: either make quantity a derived view (sum of layers) or add a reconciliation job + alert when they diverge.
close_reason: Verified: not a real bug. StockLevel.quantity and cost-layer sums are mutated together inside the same Prisma $transaction at every site (allocation.ts:1303-1310 + 1327-1329, manufacturing.ts:684-705 + 722-738 + 780-798, transfers.ts:405-414 + 556-578, stock.ts:689-756, purchase-orders.ts receipts). Postgres ACID prevents partial commits. Drift is only possible via out-of-band DB writes, which no app fix can prevent. A reconciliation job would be defensive observability, not a bug fix — open a separate feature request if desired.

title: FIFO consumption race: candidate read happens before per-row lock
description:
lib/cost-layers.ts:62-95 — findMany reads the candidate cost-layer set, then takes per-row locks. Two concurrent shippers can read the same candidate set before either acquires a lock, then both decrement the same layer.

Impact: physical stock corruption — over-consumption, negative remainingQty, broken FIFO ordering.

VERIFY: confirm whether the lock is actually `SELECT ... FOR UPDATE` on the same row IDs and whether the surrounding $transaction is at Serializable isolation. If not, switch to Serializable or take the row locks before/inside the candidate read (e.g. `SELECT ... FOR UPDATE SKIP LOCKED`).
close_reason: Verified: not a bug. consumeFifoLayers (lib/cost-layers.ts:62-95) implements the textbook lock-then-re-read pattern: (1) findMany candidate IDs, (2) lockCostLayers acquires SELECT...FOR UPDATE on those IDs, (3) re-reads the locked rows via findMany on id IN [...] for fresh remainingQty values, (4) decrements based on the post-lock state. The explicit comment at line 69-70 documents this: 'Re-read after taking row locks so concurrent consumers cannot both act on the same pre-lock remainingQty snapshot and double-decrement a layer.' The agent missed the re-read.

title: Supplier returns: qty validation outside the tx + no idempotency key (concurrent/duplicate over-return)
description: returnPurchaseOrder validates 'can't return more than net received' BEFORE the tx; only stock_levels are FOR UPDATE locked inside. Concurrent returns from different warehouses can each pass the pre-tx check then both increment qtyReturned (over-return), and duplicate submits create extra returns + draft credit notes (no idempotency key/unique return reference). Re-validate remaining returnable under a PO-line lock inside the tx; add an idempotency key. Pre-existing; surfaced in Codex review of the return credit-note feature.

title: Return credit-note nets against ALL credit notes on the bill (manual credits can suppress return credit silently)
description: computeReturnCreditNoteDraft caps/nets the incremental credit against the SUM of all SupplierCreditNotes on the bill, including unrelated manual credits — safe (prevents over-credit) but a manual allowance can silently consume the return-credit capacity (under-credit). Consider distinguishing return-generated credits and warning when manual credits consumed the capacity. Codex MEDIUM.

title: Supplier credit-note posting uses bill-level tax type; wrong for mixed/reverse-charge PRODUCT bills
description: postSupplierCreditNote (app/actions/purchase-orders.ts ~3373) + resolveSupplierCreditNoteTaxType derive tax from bill-level taxForeign>0 + supplier tax type, built for FREIGHT. Product bills can carry per-line product tax types and reverse-charge swaps (RC bill has taxForeign=0 but goods are vatable). A product-return credit note (now auto-created) posted via this path can use the wrong tax type (e.g. NONE for an RC product), mis-posting VAT/GL. Derive tax from the returned invoice lines; split credit-note lines when tax treatment differs. Surfaced in Codex review of the return credit-note feature.

title: Auto-create DRAFT supplier credit note when returning goods from a billed PO
description: Returning items from a billed/prepaid PO leaves the AP liability standing and surfaces a misleading prepaid-reconciliation banner ('not received/not arrived/chase delivery') even though the goods arrived and were returned. User decision: auto-create a DRAFT SupplierCreditNote for the returned (over-billed) value, linked to the offsetting bill, for finance to review + post to Xero. Invoice lines are NET (ex-VAT); credit-note amountForeign is GROSS (tax-inclusive) and capped to the bill's remaining creditable. Net out existing credit notes for idempotency across multiple returns. Also fix the prepaid + over-billing banners to reflect the auto-created credit note and only frame genuine non-delivery (billed-but-never-received) as 'chase delivery'.
close_reason: Implemented + merged in PR #258 (deployed). Auto-creates DRAFT supplier credit note on return from a billed PO; prepaid banner now flags only genuine non-delivery. Codex-reviewed; follow-ups filed for product-tax-type posting, manual-credit visibility, concurrent-return idempotency.

title: Receive/Return PO dialogs show a horizontal scrollbar (table wider than dialog)
description: The redesigned Receive Goods / Return Items dialogs use a table with min-w-[680px] inside an overflow-x-auto card. ProductLink's name span can't truncate because the Product column isn't width-bounded, so long product names push the table past the dialog's inner width -> horizontal scrollbar. Fix: table-fixed w-full (table always equals dialog width) with the Product cell truncating; drop min-w + overflow-x-auto; make qty input w-full so it fits its fixed cell.
close_reason: Fixed in PR #257 (deployed). table-fixed w-full + truncating Product cell.

title: Verify manufacturing PRODUCTION_OUT movements create required cogs_entries
description: Codex review of acf5464 observed that manufacturing.ts PRODUCTION_OUT writers (~645, ~744) may not create cogs_entries, while migration 20260602103000 trigger requires PRODUCTION_OUT rows to have a cogs_entries row (migration.sql ~171). If real, completing/disassembling a manufacturing order would fail the evidence guard. Verify against current code (entries may be created later in the loop) and add a regression test. Unrelated to the purchasing return fix; surfaced during its review.

title: FIFO shortfall tolerance can leave stock_movement.qty > consumed qty (reporting-value CHECK risk for fractional consumption)
description: consumeFifoLayersStrict only throws when the unconsumed remainder > 0.0001 (lib/domain/inventory/cost-layers.ts ~229), so for FRACTIONAL quantities sum(consumed.qty) can be up to 0.0001 less than the requested qty. Callers (returnPurchaseOrder, sales shipment-service, transfers out, manufacturing) set the stock_movements row qty = requested qty but build reporting value fields from the consumed layers (qty = consumed sum). buildStockMovementValueFieldsFromTotal now stores totalValueBase = ROUND(consumedQty * unitCostBase, 6); the DB CHECK stock_movements_reporting_value_consistent evaluates ROUND(rowQty * unitCostBase, 6). When rowQty != consumedQty (fractional shortfall), these can differ and re-trigger the violation. Integer quantities are unaffected (no shortfall) so the common case is fixed by acf5464. Robust fix: reconcile the row qty with the actually-consumed qty (or pass rowQty into the value builder) across all FIFO-consuming writers. Pre-existing; surfaced during Codex review of acf5464.

title: PO line-items thumbnail squeezed into a vertical strip on narrow/multi-column tables
description: ProductThumb's has fixed h-9 w-9 but no max-w-none. Tailwind Preflight applies img{max-width:100%}, so when the PO detail Order Lines table (min-w-[800px], no overflow wrapper) is squeezed below the thumbnail's 36px column on a constrained viewport or a multi-column (foreign-currency + landed-cost) PO, the img WIDTH collapses to the cell while HEIGHT stays 36px -> thin vertical strip. Became visible after the staging dev server restart picked up the wider max-w-6xl PO detail layout (#252). Fix: add max-w-none + shrink-0 to the ProductThumb img (and harden the placeholder) so it stays a fixed 36px square everywhere it's used.
close_reason: Fixed in PR #256 (squashed to development, FF main @ 3a395cb, deployed to staging). max-w-none+shrink-0 on ProductThumb img.

title: PO detail totals misaligned: footer labelSpan off by one (omits thumbnail column)
description: In po-detail-client.tsx the main line-items tfoot computes labelSpan = 3 + conditionals to place Subtotal/VAT/Total amounts under the 'Total (currency)' header, but the table has 4 always-present leading columns (thumbnail w-12, Product, Qty, Unit Cost) — the thumbnail column is omitted. So the foreign subtotal/total amounts render one column to the left of the currency header. Fix: base labelSpan at 4.
close_reason: PR #254 merged — footer labelSpan base 3→4 so PO totals align under the Total currency column.

title: Reorder auto-PO note goes to customer-visible 'notes' instead of internalNotes
description: createReorderPOs (app/actions/forecasting.ts) sets notes: REORDER_PO_NOTE on the draft PO (the supplier/customer-visible field, rendered on the RFQ/PO PDF). It should set internalNotes instead. The dedup query (where: { notes: REORDER_PO_NOTE }) must also move to internalNotes.
close_reason: PR #252 merged — reorder auto-PO note moved to internalNotes (dedup matches both during transition); PO detail widened to max-w-6xl + 4-col header grid.

title: Product lead time auto-derived from PO history with manual override
description: Add Product.leadTimeDays (manual override) + Product.observedLeadTimeDays (auto P95 from PO receipts, daily cron). Effective = manual ?? observed ?? 14. Reorder report uses product-level effective lead time (drops live per-supplier P95 scan). Editable 'Lead time (days)' on product form (blank=auto). Cron recompute-product-lead-times + initial backfill. Plan: wondrous-singing-beaver.md.
close_reason: PR #251 merged — product lead time auto-derived from PO P95 + manual override; backfill pending on staging.

title: WC variants should inherit their parent product's category (sync + backfill)
description: syncVariations creates/updates VARIANT products without a categoryId, so variants are uncategorised. Make variants inherit the parent VARIABLE product's resolved category: pass parent imsCategoryId into syncVariations (set on create + update), and extend the category backfill to propagate parent.categoryId to existing variants. Follow-up to az15/k2uj.
close_reason: PR #250 merged; sync makes variants inherit parent category, backfill applied to staging (1507 variants).

title: Backfill: re-resolve IMS product categories from WC primary category for already-imported products
description: One-off script to re-resolve categoryId for existing WC-linked products using the new primary-category logic (az15). Pages WC /products, resolves via resolveImsCategoryId (primary→deepest), matches IMS by externalProductId (fallback sku), updates only changed categoryId. Dry-run by default; APPLY=1 to write. Scope matches the import: SIMPLE/VARIABLE products (variants don't carry category in the import).
close_reason: PR #249 merged; backfill applied to staging (169 categories re-resolved, idempotent, no clears). Script hardened for prod reuse.

title: WC product import: map the Yoast/RankMath primary category to IMS category (not deepest)
description: When a WC product has multiple categories, product-sync picks the deepest category via pickDeepestImsCategoryId, which can mis-assign or leave IMS category empty. Use the WC primary category (Yoast _yoast_wpseo_primary_product_cat, RankMath rank_math_primary_product_cat in product meta_data) resolved through the category mirror; fall back to deepest when no primary is set/mapped. Example: SKU 4923239082 has 7 categories with primary cat 233 (Hotends).
close_reason: PR #248 merged — WC primary category (Yoast/RankMath) mapped to IMS category, deepest fallback.

title: Reorder report Warehouse availability shows warehouse ID cuid instead of name
description: availabilityBreakdownByProduct in replenishment-reports.ts formats the breakdown as '<warehouseId>: <qty>' using the raw cuid (e.g. cmq8hpv...: 1). Join warehouse name/code and display that, falling back to id. Keep integer qty.
close_reason: PR #247 merged — Warehouse availability shows warehouse name (code/id fallback).

title: Pre-existing: Create-PO/MO buttons use legacy generateForecasts, not the Reorder Planning report
description: createReorderPOs (app/actions/forecasting.ts) builds draft-PO quantities from generateForecasts (the legacy statistical engine: recommendedOrderQty, old critical/low urgency vocabulary), and createReorderMOs re-runs getReorderReport({}) with DEFAULT filters — neither uses the reorder row the user sees (with the new order-up-to integer qty, the page's targetCoverWeeks/demand-window/warehouse filters, inbound netting, etc.). So PO/MO quantities can diverge from the displayed Reorder Planning rows. Pre-existing (predates the order-up-to + integer-rounding work, 5f19/32cl). Fix: drive PO/MO creation from getReorderReport rows with the page's filters (or pass validated row quantities into the action). Also keep qty as Decimal/validated-integer rather than Number() in reorder-mo-planning (precision). Surfaced by Codex during PR review of 5f19.
close_reason: PR #246 merged — Create-PO/MO now source quantities from getReorderReport with the page filters; PO selection extracted to a tested pure helper with skip reasons + server-side filter sanitization.

title: Reorder report: compute ABC by demand volume + show ABC column
description: The reorder report's ABC filter read the stored product.abcClass (mostly null) so it returned nothing. Replace with a dynamic Pareto ABC by units sold in the demand window (top 80% movement=A, next 15%=B, rest=C; zero-movement defaults to C) using already-loaded velocity data (no new query). Also add an ABC column to the reorder table. User-chosen approach: auto-compute by demand volume.
close_reason: PR #242 merged — ABC computed by demand volume + column added.

title: Consolidate Reorder Forecast into Reorder Planning + retire forecast page
description: Bring the historical-sales import (WC+CSV Training dialog) and the ABC-class/urgency/text-search filters from the orphaned /analytics/forecast into the linked /analytics/reorder, then redirect /analytics/forecast→/analytics/reorder. Filters applied server-side in getReorderReport before sort/totals/pagination. Import dialog extracted to a shared component, mounted on reorder via a sync-permission-gated trigger (FINANCE lacks sync). Keep generateForecasts/forecast settings/createReorderPOs/export route (still referenced; retention feeds purge cron). Drop the forecast SettingsDialog UI. Plan: /root/.claude/plans/wondrous-singing-beaver.md
close_reason: PR #241 merged — forecast consolidated into reorder, old page retired.

title: Unified tax-rate mapping in onboarding wizard + Settings (WC/Xero/IMS, rate-first matching)
description: Move the wizard Tax step after Integrations; add a unified mapper that imports WooCommerce + Xero tax rates, auto-matches to IMS canonical rates by rate% first then normalized name, lets manual override, and creates IMS rates for unmatched WC rates. Shared component replaces the Settings WC tax tab + Xero tax tab. QuickBooks out of scope. Plan: /root/.claude/plans/wondrous-singing-beaver.md. New: lib/tax/tax-rate-match.ts (pure matcher), components/settings/unified-tax-rate-mapper.tsx, getTaxRateMatchData/applyTaxRateMatches actions. Reuse importWcTaxRatesFromApi, ShoppingTaxRateMapping CRUD, fetchXeroTaxRates, getTaxRates/createTaxRate/updateTaxRate. Reorder wizard STEPS + refactor step render blocks to key-based.
close_reason: PR #235 merged. Unified WC/Xero/IMS tax-rate mapper in onboarding wizard (tax step moved after integrations; render blocks key-based; key-based progress persistence) + Settings WooCommerce Tax tab. Rate-first matcher (lib/tax/tax-rate-match.ts). Codex-reviewed (2 rounds): auto-apply no-clobber, WC unmap restored, key-based onboarding, determinism, e2e aligned — approve.

title: Help portal: onboarding-walkthrough / glossary / troubleshooting registered but .md files missing (dead links)
description: app/actions/help.ts HELP_DOCS registers slugs onboarding-walkthrough, glossary, troubleshooting, but no corresponding help-docs/*.md exist, so getHelpDocs() filters them out and getHelpDoc() returns null. getting-started.md (and stock-control/dashboard/analytics docs) link to glossary.md#lifecycle-status / troubleshooting.md / onboarding-walkthrough.md → dead links. Fix: author the three help docs (wizard walkthrough of the 9 steps; glossary incl. lifecycle-status, FIFO, COGS, EOL, stock levels, landed cost, reverse-charge VAT; troubleshooting incl. connection-test gate, encryption key, step-up reauth) + ensure docs/ symlinks resolve them.
close_reason: PR #234 merged. (4t5q) outbox naming test regex relaxed to allow hyphenated letter-led segments — full suite green. (9aph) added docs/ symlinks for onboarding-walkthrough/glossary/troubleshooting; in-app help already served them from help-docs/, the docs/ mirror just lacked the symlinks.

title: Settings encryption key: accept 64-char hex + actionable error + centralize resolver
description: resolveSettingsEncryptionKey (lib/security/encrypted-settings.ts) and resolveEncryptionKey (lib/secrets.ts) are byte-identical and accept ONLY base64-decoding-to-32 or raw-utf8-32. A 64-hex-char key (openssl rand -hex 32 — a very common format, and what staging's ENCRYPTION_KEY was) decodes to 48 bytes → rejected → null → throws 'SETTINGS_ENCRYPTION_KEY is required...' even though a key IS set. The error is non-actionable (doesn't say the key is present-but-wrong-format or how to generate one). Fix: centralize into a shared parseAesKey/resolveAesEncryptionKey that ALSO accepts 64-char hex; make the thrown errors actionable (mention openssl rand -base64 32 + accepted formats); align lib/ops/production-preflight.ts to accept hex. Surfaced when WooCommerce save in onboarding tried to encrypt wc_consumer_secret on staging (staging unblocked separately by adding a base64 SETTINGS_ENCRYPTION_KEY).
close_reason: PR #232 merged. Settings encryption key resolver centralized + accepts 64-char hex (and base64-32/raw-32); actionable error distinguishes absent vs present-but-wrong-format; preflight + .env.example aligned. Codex security review: approve (no byte-meaning change for existing keys). Staging unblocked separately with a base64 key.

title: Outbox registry naming test fails: 'landed-cost.adjustment-journal' vs regex ^[a-z]+\.[a-z-]+$
description: tests/domain/integrations/outbox.test.ts:284 asserts operation strings match /^[a-z]+\.[a-z-]+$/, but the grob landed-cost journal outbop op 'landed-cost.adjustment-journal' has a hyphen in the namespace segment ('landed-cost'), so the test fails. Pre-existing on development since PR #223 (grob). Fix: either relax the regex to allow hyphens in the namespace (/^[a-z][a-z-]*\.[a-z-]+$/) OR rename the operation (breaking — operation is persisted in IntegrationOutbox rows/idempotency keys, so prefer the regex relaxation + test update). Not auth-related; found while running the full unit suite during the step-up PR.
close_reason: PR #234 merged. (4t5q) outbox naming test regex relaxed to allow hyphenated letter-led segments — full suite green. (9aph) added docs/ symlinks for onboarding-walkthrough/glossary/troubleshooting; in-app help already served them from help-docs/, the docs/ mirror just lacked the symlinks.

title: [audit-C1b] Prepaid PO billed-vs-received reconciliation alert
description:
Follow-up to C1 (onetwo3d-ims-32tm). C1 enforces bill<=received for pay-on-receipt suppliers and bill<=ordered for prepaid suppliers (Supplier.prepaid). But prepaid suppliers lose all billed-exceeds-received protection: prepay/bill 100, receive 90 → 10 units paid for but never arrived, with no signal. The three-way-match block is intentionally off for prepaid (the deposit was deliberate), so this needs a soft alert, not a block.

Fix: when a prepaid-supplier PO reaches fully-received (or on the PO detail page as a read-time computed banner), compare total billed qty vs total received qty per line. If billed > received, surface an amber 'Prepaid reconciliation' banner + WARNING activity log naming the delta and value ('N units / £X prepaid but not received — claim a supplier credit'). If billed < received (under-prepaid), note the balancing bill due. Reuses the rejected-sync-warnings / multi-component-warning alert pattern.

Automating the actual supplier credit memo for the short delta is out of scope here — coordinate with C4 (onetwo3d-ims-pbu4) which builds the 'supplier owes us' credit-memo machinery.

Acceptance: a prepaid PO billed 100 / received 90 shows the reconciliation banner with delta 10 and the prepaid value; pay-on-receipt POs (which can't over-bill) never show it; a fully-reconciled prepaid PO (billed==received) shows nothing. Read-time computation preferred (no new write path in the receive transaction).
close_reason: Fixed in PR #205. Pure computePrepaidReconciliation (prepaid suppliers only; billed − net-received per line, value ex-VAT) drives a read-time amber 'Prepaid reconciliation' banner on the PO detail when billed>received, gated to receipt-complete statuses. Returns netted so the figure matches C4; C4 over-billing banner suppressed for prepaid POs (one banner). Under-billed (received>billed) shows a blue 'balancing bill due' note. Codex caught returns-not-netted/double-banner (HIGH) + unrendered hasUnderBilled (HIGH) + mid-delivery firing; all hardened. 8 helper tests.

title: [audit-H9] Editing an adjustment whose layers were since consumed deletes old COGS then fails
description:
updateAdjustmentMovement (app/actions/stock.ts:643-810) deletes the old COGS entries/unlocks layers, then re-runs consumeFifoLayersStrict at the new quantity. If a later sale consumed those layers, the re-consumption throws AFTER the deletion — old COGS gone, new impossible, adjustment left half-edited.

Fix: validate feasibility BEFORE mutating: dry-run the FIFO consumption at the new quantity against current layer state inside the same tx; only if it succeeds, delete old entries and apply. On infeasibility return a clear error ('layers consumed by later movements — reverse those first or create a compensating adjustment').

Acceptance: infeasible edits are rejected atomically with the old COGS intact; test simulates a post-adjustment sale then an edit.
close_reason: Fixed in PR #195. updateAdjustmentMovement now validates FIFO feasibility (pure assertAdjustmentEditFifoFeasible: pool = currentRemaining + restorableConsumed − removableLayer, matched to strict's 0.0001 tolerance + remainingQty>0 filter) and hoists the layer/cogs precondition guards ABOVE all mutation, so infeasible edits are rejected atomically with a clear message and zero mutation instead of delete-then-rollback. Race fallback re-throws the actionable message. Codex caught epsilon mismatch (HIGH), float-sum drift, race UX; all hardened. 9/9 helper + 18/18 stock/cost-layer tests.

title: [audit-H7] Receiving allows divergence from PO destination warehouse with no warning
description:
Per-line warehouse choice at receive time is a feature, but nothing warns when the chosen warehouse differs from po.destinationWarehouseId, and landed-cost distribution assumes the planned location. Mis-clicks send stock+layers to the wrong site silently.

Fix: in the receive dialog, visually flag lines whose warehouse differs from the PO destination (amber border + inline note); require a confirm checkbox when ANY line diverges; include the divergence in the receipt activity log metadata.

Acceptance: receiving to a non-destination warehouse requires explicit confirmation and is logged; default flow (matching warehouse) unchanged.
close_reason: Fixed in PR #203. Pure findDivergentReceiptLines + receivePurchaseOrder rejects divergent receipts unless options.confirmWarehouseDivergence (logs divergence in receipt metadata). Receive dialog: amber-bordered select + '≠ destination' note on divergent lines, confirm checkbox gating Confirm (resets on edit), passes the flag. Codex caught WMS booked-in path bypassing the guard (HIGH) — now logs received_warehouse_divergence WARNING; + stale-consent reset. 5/5 helper + 14/14 WMS + 94/94 purchasing tests.

title: [audit-H6] BOM edits between IN_PROGRESS and COMPLETED create ghost reservations
description:
Component reservation at IN_PROGRESS and consumption at COMPLETED both read the live BOM (app/actions/manufacturing.ts:946-952, 588-626). Editing the BOM in between (e.g. 4 legs→3) leaves the delta reserved forever with no owning order.

Fix: snapshot the component requirements (componentId, qty) onto the production order at the IN_PROGRESS transition (JSON column or child rows); consumption and release both read the snapshot, never the live BOM. Editing a BOM with IN_PROGRESS orders gets a warning naming them.

Acceptance: BOM edit mid-production no longer changes consumption; release on cancel returns exactly what was reserved; test covers the edit-in-between scenario.
close_reason: Fixed in PR #196. ProductionOrder.componentSnapshot (JSONB) frozen at IN_PROGRESS (fresh read under FOR UPDATE); completion/cancellation/release/logs/detail-view/stock-sync all resolve components from the snapshot (parseProductionOrderComponentSnapshot, rejects qty<=0), falling back to live BOM only when no snapshot. reserve==consume==release set, so mid-production BOM edits can't strand reservations or change consumption. saveProductComponents warns naming in-progress orders (UI + log). Codex caught stale-read snapshot (HIGH), missing cancel lock (HIGH), live-BOM logs/detail (MEDIUM), qty<=0 (MEDIUM); all hardened. 48/48 manufacturing tests. Migration 20260613000000.

title: [audit-H8] PO cancellation leaves COGS from already-sold units standing with no finance flag
description:
reversePurchaseOrderCostLayersForCancellation only reverses remainingQty; units already consumed keep COGS sourced from a now-cancelled PO. Inventory reconciles; P&L silently carries cost from a cancelled receipt.

Fix: at cancellation, compute consumedQty per affected layer; if non-zero, emit a WARNING activity log + include in the cancellation result so the UI shows 'N units already sold from this PO — COGS of £X remains booked; review with finance'. Optionally queue a COGS-correction journal when the operator confirms the goods are being un-bought (returned to supplier) rather than just the order being closed.

Acceptance: cancelling a partially-consumed PO surfaces the consumed quantity and value; activity log metadata is machine-readable.
close_reason: Fixed in PR #194 (phase-1 alert-only). C4: returnPurchaseOrder computes over-billing (billed qty vs kept) inside the tx, emits return_overbilled_bill WARNING + amber PO-page alert naming the contributing bills and over-billed amount. H8: cancellation reads consumed = received − remaining BEFORE reversal, emits cancelled_consumed_cogs_standing WARNING + amber post-cancel banner with units sold + COGS still booked. Codex pass caught 2 HIGH (post-commit race read, unguarded WARNING failing a committed cancel) + 4 MEDIUM, all hardened. 88/88 purchasing tests.

title: [audit-H4] Switching accounting connector strands queued sync rows
description:
Sync rows are stamped with their connector at queue time. Switching Xero→QBO leaves PENDING xero rows invisible to both processors — silent permanent accumulation.

Fix: on connector switch (integration plugin toggle), surface a blocking confirmation listing pending rows for the outgoing connector with options: process-before-switch / mark-cancelled / leave (with a persistent Sync Dashboard warning chip while orphans exist). Add a dashboard tile counting cross-connector orphans.

Acceptance: switching with pending rows requires explicit operator choice; orphan count visible on the sync dashboard; orphaned rows can be bulk-cancelled with activity log.
close_reason: Fixed in PR #201. summarizeCrossConnectorOrphans (pure) + getCrossConnectorOrphanSummary (groupBy live rows vs active connector) + cancelOrphanedAccountingSyncRows (bulk FAILED w/ WARNING log, staleness-guarded against mid-flight, rejects all-wipe when no active connector) + ConnectorOrphanBanner on the sync dashboard (per-connector + cancel-all). Connector-agnostic; no QBO-specific processing per constraint. Codex caught mid-flight race (HIGH) + no-active-connector all-wipe (HIGH); hardened. Filed follow-up for CANCELLED status in reconciliation. 4/4 tests.

title: [audit-H5] SALES/PURCHASE_INVOICE_UPDATE can process before its CREATE
description:
An UPDATE can be queued while the original CREATE is still PENDING; the processor has ordering protection for INVOICE_PAYMENT but not for CREATE→UPDATE. UPDATE fails with 'invoice not found', burns retries, fills the failed surface.

Fix: in the processor, when handling *_INVOICE_UPDATE, check for an earlier live (PENDING/PROCESSING) CREATE row for the same referenceId and defer (skip without retry-count increment) until it is SYNCED — same pattern as findInvoicePaymentsBlockedByEarlierLiveLogs.

Acceptance: queueing CREATE then UPDATE in the same cron window processes them in order with no failed attempts; test covers the deferral.
close_reason: Fixed in PR #198 (Xero). New findInvoiceUpdatesBlockedByPendingCreate() defers SALES_INVOICE_UPDATE/PURCHASE_INVOICE_UPDATE when a live PENDING/PROCESSING CREATE of the matching type exists for the same (referenceType, referenceId), via deferUpdateUntilCreatePosts (PENDING + 60s gate, no retryCount increment). Wired into both direct + outbox loops, mirroring the INVOICE_PAYMENT ordering guard. Codex review: removed dead check, clarified createdAt<= boundary, added same-ms + later-create boundary tests; verified no outbox exhaustion (resetAttempts). 9/9 sync-processor tests.

title: [audit-H2] Payment recorded without invoice when invoice trigger is manual
description:
addPayment only generates an invoice number when invoice_trigger='on_paid'. With trigger=manual, a fully-paid order can sit with a recorded receivable and no invoice ever generated or synced — GL receivable/invoice mismatch.

Fix: when an order becomes fully paid and has no invoiceNumber, emit a WARNING activity log + surface an amber chip on the order detail ('Paid without invoice — generate now') with a one-click generate action. Do NOT auto-generate (manual trigger means the operator wants control), just make the gap loud.

Acceptance: paying an uninvoiced manual-trigger order surfaces the alert; generating clears it; activity log carries metadata.
close_reason: Fixed in PR #202 (alert-only). Pure shouldWarnPaidWithoutInvoice (warns when fully paid + no invoice + trigger not on_paid/on_shipped). addPayment + markSalesOrderPaid emit paid_without_invoice WARNING (addPayment re-reads invoiceNumber to avoid a stale-read spurious log). SO detail shows amber 'Paid without invoice — generate now' chip gated server-side on the same trigger-aware helper, suppressing the plain generate button + pending-sync chip so there's one affordance. Codex caught chip-ignored-trigger (HIGH) + UI collisions; hardened. Filed follow-ups for on_shipped-cancelled gap + markSalesOrderPaid lock. 7/7 helper + 99/99 sales tests.

title: [audit-H3] Sync success + back-reference write failure orphans the external ID permanently
description:
In lib/accounting.ts + xero/sync-processor.ts: if the Xero push succeeds but updateBackReference (writing accountingInvoiceId onto the order/PO) fails, the sync row is SYNCED, idempotency blocks re-push, and the document never learns its external id. No repair path exists.

Fix: persist externalTransactionId on the sync row BEFORE attempting the back-reference; add a repair sweep (cron or on-demand) that finds SYNCED rows whose referenced document lacks the external id and re-applies the back-reference from the stored value. Make updateBackReference failures mark the row NEEDS_BACKREF instead of leaving silent divergence.

Acceptance: killing the process between push and back-reference leaves a row the repair sweep fixes on next run; test simulates the failure with a mock that throws on back-reference.
close_reason: Fixed in PR #197 (Xero). updateBackReference no longer swallows write errors — failures propagate to the caller's retry (re-applies from already-persisted externalTransactionId via the entry.externalTransactionId branch). New repairXeroBackReferences() sweep (cron + manual) re-applies back-refs for SYNCED/FAILED rows whose doc still lacks the id AND re-enqueues follow-ups, resetting FAILED→SYNCED; skips ambiguous multi-bill POs. Pure back-reference.ts module (applyBackReference throws, backReferenceIsMissing, syncTypeWritesBackReference) DI-seam tested incl. mock-throws-on-backref. Codex caught missing follow-up enqueue (HIGH), missing payload select (HIGH), multi-bill id-swap (HIGH); all hardened. 40/40 accounting/xero.

title: [audit-H1] Credit notes skip the reverse-charge tax-type swap
description:
queueRefundAccountingActions (app/actions/sales.ts ~1502-1528) builds the credit-note payload with the original taxType and never consults TaxRate.reverseCharge / settings.reverseChargeSalesTaxType — the swap shipped for invoices and bills in PR #183 is missing on the refund path. A reverse-charged sale refunded posts the credit note under the standard code; VAT return debits and credits use different tax types.

Fix: apply the same swap used in queueSalesInvoiceForOrder: when the line's taxRate.reverseCharge is true and the setting is non-empty, substitute the configured code. Select reverseCharge on the refund path's tax-rate reads.

Acceptance: a refund of a reverse-charged order produces a credit-note payload whose lines carry the swapped tax type; helper/unit test asserts payload shape; non-reverse-charge refunds unchanged.
close_reason: Fixed in PR #192: credit-note product lines now apply the per-line reverse-charge tax-type swap via shared lib/accounting/reverse-charge.ts:resolveSalesLineTaxType (same resolver as the sales-invoice push). Codex adversarial pass caught a shipping-fallback asymmetry; hardened so shipping/unmapped lines follow the order-level type unswapped on both invoice and credit note. 6 helper tests. WC-import shipping RC gap filed as r3xh.2.

title: Surface rejected Xero invoice updates in IMS
description: When Xero rejects an invoice update after IMS queues SALES_INVOICE_UPDATE or PURCHASE_INVOICE_UPDATE, the failure remains in AccountingSyncLog/activity but operators do not get a clear message at the relevant IMS record. Rejected updates can happen when the external invoice/bill is paid, locked, voided, or otherwise no longer editable in Xero. IMS should surface this as an actionable warning/message on the sales order or purchase invoice context instead of leaving users to find it in low-level sync logs.
acceptance_criteria: Rejected SALES_INVOICE_UPDATE and PURCHASE_INVOICE_UPDATE sync attempts are visible from the related IMS record with connector, error message, timestamp, and retry/remediation guidance. Tests cover a rejected update row being surfaced without exposing raw sensitive payload data.
close_reason: fixed

title: Audit remaining endOfUtcDay callers for half-open UTC date filter conversion
description:
PR #166 added a JSDoc warning on endOfUtcDay about PostgreSQL microsecond precision and converted 2 sites (inventory-ledger-reports.ts dateRangeWhere, stock-position-reports.ts getNegativeStockReport) to the half-open lt: startOfNextUtcDay(...) pattern. A grep of the codebase surfaces additional callers that may have the same latent issue:

- lib/domain/inventory/get-on-hand-as-of.ts:714 - 'gt: endOfUtcDay(priorSnapshotDate), lte: asOf'. The 'gt: endOfUtcDay(...)' creates the INVERSE bug — a movement at prior:23:59:59.999500us would be included even though it's part of the prior day.
- lib/domain/inventory/get-on-hand-as-of.ts:736 - 'lte: endOfUtcDay(futureSnapshotDate)' — same bug as the two fixed.
- lib/domain/inventory/inventory-snapshot.ts:904, :1027, :1064, :1072, :1117 — multiple endOfUtcDay uses as cutoffs/boundaries; each needs per-call semantics audit (which side of the cutoff is inclusive, where the rounding error matters).

Audit each, convert to lt: startOfNextUtcDay(...) where the upper bound should be exclusive-of-next-day, or to gte: startOfUtcDay(nextDay) where the lower bound should be inclusive-of-day-zero. Add a test pair per converted site that exercises the precision boundary the same way tests/domain/inventory/inventory-ledger-reports.test.ts does (fixture at xx:23:59:59.999Z 'should include' / xx+1:00:00:00.000Z 'should exclude').
acceptance_criteria: All endOfUtcDay callers in lib/domain/inventory/get-on-hand-as-of.ts and lib/domain/inventory/inventory-snapshot.ts either (a) converted to the half-open startOfNextUtcDay pattern with a precision-boundary test, or (b) explicitly documented with a code comment why the inclusive endOfUtcDay is correct for that callsite. No lte: endOfUtcDay(...) remains in domain queries.
close_reason: Fixed: inventory as-of replay and inventory snapshot backfill now use half-open UTC day boundaries (gte/lt next-day starts) instead of endOfUtcDay query cutoffs, with boundary regression coverage.

title: updatePurchaseOrder: recompute base-currency totals when only currency changes
description: PR #160 added FX rate re-resolution to updatePurchaseOrder so changing currency or fxRateToBase refreshes the stored rate. However, the dependent base-currency totals (directFreightBase, line subtotalBase, totalBase, etc.) are only recomputed inside the input.lines and input.additionalCosts branches. An API caller that submits a partial update with only { currency: 'EUR' } persists a new fxRateToBase but leaves directFreightBase and line *Base values calculated against the OLD rate. The UI likely forces a re-entry, but the action-level inconsistency is real and silent. Either always rebuild line+freight base totals when shouldRefreshFxRate is true, or reject partial currency-only updates with a clear error.
acceptance_criteria: updatePurchaseOrder either (a) recomputes directFreightBase + line totals when fxRateToBase is refreshed without lines/additionalCosts inputs, OR (b) rejects partial currency-only updates with an explicit 'currency change requires re-entry of lines and additional costs' error. Integration test asserts the chosen behavior.
close_reason: Fixed: currency/rate-only PO edits now rebuild order base totals, line base values, and freight cost base values from persisted foreign amounts using the refreshed FX rate, with coverage for persisted line/freight updates.

title: Update release-blocker epic (dhk) description after PR #157 merge — 9 of 16 deps will close
description: PR #157 closes 9 of the 16 dependencies of onetwo3d-ims-dhk (release 1.0 deployment blockers): 2it, 8vp, ecp, fpr, v6z, vme, xin, xjj, xx4. After merge, dhk has 7 remaining deps: P0 — a8u (FIFO/supplier-invoice reconciliation), hyz (per-line VAT vs order-level discount), ahu (realised FX gain/loss + AR/AP revaluation, blocked by a8u); P1 — imn (cost-layer-snapshot audit), zzd (PO FX rate auto-lookup), 4jz (refund idempotency test), 67x (PO cancel idempotency test). Refresh the dhk description so the remaining-scope summary at the top of the epic body matches reality.
acceptance_criteria: onetwo3d-ims-dhk description body lists exactly the 7 remaining open dependencies grouped by P0/P1 once PR #157 is merged and bd dolt push lands.
close_reason: Fixed: release-blocker epic dhk description now reflects the final fully drained blocker tree and lists all 16 closed dependencies with the final validation summary.

title: PR #157 review: expand PR description to enumerate 34 undocumented closures (especially the 4 substantive onboarding fixes)
description: PR #157 'Close fixed bd review issues' lists 15 closures in the body but the diff closes 49 issues. 34 closures are undocumented: 20 are local-bd-catching-up (NEW-IN-PR), 10 are reconcile-after-importing-codex/dead-stock-report bookkeeping, and 4 are substantive 'Fixed:' closures that should have been in the body — 8o5 (onboarding CRON_SECRET guidance), a08 (DRAFT/ACTIVE/EOL/ARCHIVED lifecycle docs), mzs (connection-test-gate onboarding UX), tan (wc_webhook_secret reuse docs). Reviewer auditability is reduced when substantive closures ride along inside a 'bd state' PR.
acceptance_criteria: PR #157 body lists all 49 closures broken down into the three groups, or splits the 4 substantive onboarding closures into their own follow-up PR with the underlying code references.
close_reason: Fixed: merged PR #157 body now enumerates all 49 closed bd rows, grouped into original listed closures, four substantive onboarding/product closures, local bookkeeping closures, and imported review-row reconciliations.

title: Phase 4: WC product sync mirrors category tree
description: Prefetch wp-json/wc/v3/products/categories (paginated) once per product-sync run. Build wcId -> imsId map by upserting each node with the right parentId. Each synced product's categoryId becomes the deepest WC category it carries. Renames in WC update IMS names by wcId.
close_reason: Phase 4 in commit 1c4b944 — WC categories prefetched + mirrored + linked to product on each sync.

title: Phase 3: searchable combobox in product form
description: Replace categoryName free-text input with a path-rendering combobox. Search across full paths. 'Create new' shows path preview and either creates flat or under selected parent. Falls back to current category if nothing matches.
close_reason: Phase 3 in commit aafad17 — full path everywhere in inventory UI; resolver handles path strings end-to-end.

title: Phase 2: /settings/inventory/categories tree UI
description: New settings page with expandable tree, inline rename, Add sub-category, Move to parent (combobox), Delete (children promote). Server actions from Phase 1.
close_reason: Implemented in commit a6a3cf3 — categories card on settings/inventory with full CRUD.

title: Phase 1: path-aware ProductCategory resolver + CRUD actions
description: Extend resolveProductCategoryIdByName to accept '>'-delimited path strings (walk and upsert each segment with parentId). Add app/actions/categories.ts with listCategoryTree, createCategory, renameCategory, moveCategory, deleteCategory (children promote one level). Unit tests cover path parsing, repeat-leaf-under-different-parents, normalization, cycle prevention on move.
close_reason: Implemented in commit 8d0fce0. Sub-issue done before epic.

title: Nested product categories: dropdown + settings + WC sync (epic)
description: Replace the product form's free-text category field with a searchable nested dropdown; add a settings page to manage the category tree; and have WooCommerce product sync mirror WC's category tree into IMS, auto-creating missing categories. Identity is (parentId, normalizedName) so leaf names can repeat under different parents (Apparel > T-Shirts vs Promo > T-Shirts). Path delimiter for user-visible strings and CSV is '>'. Deleting a category moves its children up one level rather than orphaning; products keep their lowest still-existing ancestor.
acceptance_criteria: (1) Path-aware resolver upserts category chains by '>'-separated path. (2) /settings/inventory/categories exposes a tree CRUD UI. (3) Product form uses a searchable combobox showing full path; supports creating a new node inline. (4) WC product sync prefetches WC's category tree, mirrors it into ProductCategory, and links the product to its deepest WC category. (5) All paths show as 'A > B > C' in UI/CSV. Existing flat categories survive without migration.
close_reason: All four phases complete: 8d0fce0 (resolver + actions), a6a3cf3 (settings UI), aafad17 (form/filter path), 1c4b944 (WC sync mirror).

title: Help section 404s: new docs not in allowlist + .md links not rewritten
description:
Two bugs blocking the in-app help section:

1. app/actions/help.ts hard-codes HELP_DOCS to 14 slugs. The glossary, onboarding-walkthrough, and troubleshooting articles added by the docs refresh (commits 5a068a16 + c1a375ec) are silently filtered out by getHelpDocs() and rejected by getHelpDoc(), so /help/glossary etc. return 404.

2. app/(dashboard)/help/[slug]/help-client.tsx renders markdown via react-markdown without a custom <a> renderer. Inline links written as [Glossary](glossary.md) resolve in the browser to /help/glossary.md, which does not match the /help/[slug] route, so every cross-article link in the new docs 404s. Anchor variants like glossary.md#lifecycle-status are also broken.
acceptance_criteria: getHelpDocs / getHelpDoc include glossary, onboarding-walkthrough, troubleshooting. Markdown links of the form (slug.md) and (slug.md#anchor) navigate to /help/slug and /help/slug#anchor respectively. External http(s) links and pure anchor (#) links are preserved unchanged. Verified by opening each new article in /help and clicking inter-article links.
close_reason: Fixed in commit 11cf53b on development. (1) glossary, onboarding-walkthrough, troubleshooting added to HELP_DOCS allowlist. (2) HelpMarkdownLink rewrites bare-relative *.md and *.md#anchor markdown links to /help/<slug> with next/link, preserving external and pure-anchor hrefs. Verified on running staging: all three new docs return 200 and rendered links serialise as /help/<slug>.

title: prisma/seed.ts seeds 'changeme123' which fails the runtime password policy
description: prisma/seed.ts line 79 hashes the literal 'changeme123' when SEED_TEST_ADMIN=true. That password fails the policy enforced by lib/security/password-policy.ts (PRs #146/#154): below 12-char minimum, missing uppercase/symbol, and matches the deny-list pattern /^changeme[a-z0-9...]*$/. A seeded admin can authenticate (login does not re-validate the stored hash) but cannot change their password through the UI without first picking one that meets policy — and any operator following the documented seeding flow ends up with a lockout-shaped admin account.
acceptance_criteria: prisma/seed.ts no longer hard-codes a weak password. Operator must set SEED_TEST_ADMIN_PASSWORD when SEED_TEST_ADMIN=true. The seed validates the supplied password against validateUserPassword() from lib/security/password-policy and fails fast with the exact policy error if it does not pass. Type-check passes.
close_reason: Fixed in commit 5449581 on development. prisma/seed.ts now requires SEED_TEST_ADMIN_PASSWORD env var when SEED_TEST_ADMIN=true and validates it against validateUserPassword(). Default seed unchanged. Smoke-tested all three paths.

title: CLI create-user uses stale 8-char password check instead of shared policy
description: scripts/cli.ts create-user enforces 'password.length < 8' but the runtime UI/API + lib/security/password-policy.ts require 12 chars + uppercase/lowercase/digit/symbol + deny-list (PRs #146/#154). A CLI-created user with a short or weak password may not be able to log in or change their password later through the UI without immediately hitting the policy. The CLI must call validateUserPassword() so all admin-creation paths share one policy.
acceptance_criteria: scripts/cli.ts create-user imports validateUserPassword from lib/security/password-policy and rejects weak passwords with the same message strings the UI shows. Type-check passes.
close_reason: Fixed in commit ee8b34b on development. scripts/cli.ts now imports validateUserPassword from lib/security/password-policy and applies it instead of the stale 8-char check, so CLI and UI/API share one policy.

title: Edge Runtime warning: lib/cron-auth.ts imports Node 'crypto' at module top level
description:
instrumentation.ts dynamically imports lib/cron-auth.ts. Next.js compiles instrumentation.ts for BOTH Node and Edge runtimes. Because cron-auth.ts has a top-level 'import { timingSafeEqual } from "crypto"', the Edge build trips:

    A Node.js module is loaded ('crypto' at line 1) which is not supported in the Edge Runtime.
    > 1 | import { timingSafeEqual } from 'crypto'

The Edge bundle only calls assertProductionCronSecretConfigured(), which is an env-var check and does not need crypto. The crypto import is only used by verifyCron() (Node-only cron routes).
design: Split lib/cron-auth.ts. Move MIN_CRON_SECRET_LENGTH and assertProductionCronSecretConfigured into a new edge-safe module lib/cron-secret-validation.ts (pure env-var inspection, no Node crypto). Re-export both from lib/cron-auth.ts for back-compat. Update instrumentation.ts to import directly from lib/cron-secret-validation.
acceptance_criteria: Edge runtime warning no longer appears in 'next dev' output for instrumentation.ts. lib/cron-auth.ts continues to expose verifyCron with timingSafeEqual semantics for cron routes. tests/security/cron-auth.test.ts continues to pass against the original assertProductionCronSecretConfigured contract.
close_reason: Fixed in commit c13b2f5 on development. Split MIN_CRON_SECRET_LENGTH and assertProductionCronSecretConfigured into lib/cron-secret-validation.ts (edge-safe), updated instrumentation.ts to import from there. lib/cron-auth.ts re-exports both for back-compat. Verified: 13/13 cron-auth tests pass, edge runtime warning removed, cron routes still return 401.

title: Onboarding products step doesn't surface PR #156 lifecycle (DRAFT/ACTIVE/EOL/ARCHIVED) or preferredSupplier fields
description:
PR #156 added `ProductLifecycleStatus` enum values `DRAFT` / `ACTIVE` / `EOL` / `ARCHIVED` and `Product.preferredSupplierId` / `preferredSupplierLocked`. The CSV product template includes the new columns:

```
'preferredSupplierId', 'preferredSupplierName', 'preferredSupplierLocked',
...
'lifecycleStatus',  // values: DRAFT/ACTIVE/EOL/ARCHIVED
```

But the onboarding products step (`components/onboarding/products-step.tsx`) wraps a generic `<CsvImportFlow action={importProductsCsv} ... />` with no explanation of:

1. **Lifecycle policy**: operators bulk-importing products that aren't yet ready for sale should mark them DRAFT (not ACTIVE). Per PR #156: 'draft products can be purchased but are not published/sellable'. Default ACTIVE assumes everything is publishable.

2. **Preferred supplier**: products imported without a preferredSupplierId won't appear in supplier-scoped reorder forecasts until the first PO_SENT auto-populates it. Operators bulk-importing without supplier info miss the P8.2 'follow real buying history' workflow.

3. **WC published status**: `shouldForceWooDraft` returns true for DRAFT and ARCHIVED — so products imported as DRAFT will be force-drafted in WC. Operators expecting their imported products to appear on the storefront won't see them until they transition to ACTIVE.

The pre-PR #156 onboarding worked because there was only one publishable state. Post PR #156, the products step needs to either:
- Explicitly guide operators on the lifecycle choice ('Are these products ready for sale immediately, or do you want to review first?')
- Provide a wizard-time bulk action: 'Set all imported products to DRAFT for now / ACTIVE'
- Surface the new fields in a 'check imported product status' summary screen after the import

**Why this matters:**

The default ACTIVE assumption was harmless when ACTIVE/NOT_FOR_SALE/ARCHIVED was the only choice. With DRAFT introduced, the default exposes operators to the 'goods arrived but they auto-published before I was ready' surprise. Onboarding is the moment operators set up bulk product data — getting the lifecycle choice right here saves cleanup later.

**Note:** the wizard already calls `importProductsCsv` which presumably honors the CSV-provided lifecycle column. The gap is the OPERATOR-FACING guidance, not the backend.
acceptance_criteria:
- Add a brief explanation of lifecycle states in the products step (with examples)
- Either: provide a wizard-time radio button 'Mark imported products as: [DRAFT for review | ACTIVE for immediate sale]' that prefills the column on import
- Or: after import completes, show a summary count by lifecycle state with an 'update all to X' option
- Update the CSV template description to mention the DRAFT default recommendation for first-time imports
- Same audit for preferredSupplierId — show post-import 'N products have no preferred supplier; first PO with supplier S will set it'
notes: Filed during onboarding audit. P8.1/P8.2 were implemented in PR #156 but the onboarding UX didn't get the corresponding refresh. The CSV template was updated; the wizard's narrative wasn't.
close_reason: Fixed: products onboarding now documents DRAFT/ACTIVE/EOL/ARCHIVED lifecycle behavior, preferredSupplierId/preferredSupplierName CSV fields, DRAFT recommendation for first imports, and preferred-supplier effects on reorder forecasts/draft POs.

title: Onboarding wizard skips PR #152 connection test gate for WooCommerce/Xero/Shopify integrations
description:
PR #152 introduced an integration connection test gate that requires a successful current-fingerprint test BEFORE enabling sync for WooCommerce, Xero, and SMTP. New actions added:
- `testWcCredentials` (app/actions/wc-sync.ts)
- `testXeroConnection` (app/actions/xero-sync.ts)  
- `testMintsoftConnection` (app/actions/mintsoft-sync.ts)

The onboarding integrations step (`components/onboarding/integrations-step.tsx`) calls the SAVE actions directly without invoking the test gate:

- `saveShoppingConnectorCredentials(...)` — WooCommerce
- `saveShopifyConnectorCredentials(...)` — Shopify
- `saveAccountingConnectionSettings(...)` — Xero / QuickBooks
- `saveMintsoftConnectionSettings(...)` — Mintsoft (inline-tests during save, so OK)

**Operator-visible failure mode:**

1. Operator completes onboarding wizard, providing WC credentials + saving.
2. Wizard reports success; operator moves to next step.
3. Later, operator tries to enable sync from /sync UI.
4. The PR #152 gate evaluates fingerprint: status='never', expectedFingerprint='abc' → returns 'Test the WooCommerce connection successfully before enabling it.'
5. Operator is confused — they thought they configured WC during onboarding.

Same flow for Xero (after OAuth completes, sync enablement is gated). For Shopify, same gate likely applies (PR #152 added shopify_webhook_secret to the gate scheme).

**Why this matters:**

The onboarding wizard's stated job is 'get IMS configured end-to-end so the operator can start using it'. Leaving sync in a NOT-ENABLED state after onboarding completes is a UX gap that the connection test gate's introduction created — but onboarding wasn't updated to match.

**Mintsoft is OK** because `saveMintsoftConnectionSettings` already calls `testMintsoftConnectionSettings` inline (saw in PR #152 review). The wizard's call satisfies the gate.

**Suggested fix:**

After each save call in the integrations step:
1. Call the corresponding test action (`testWcCredentials`, `testXeroConnection`)
2. Surface a 'connection verified' or 'connection test failed' status to the user
3. Block 'mark integration as complete' until the test passes
4. Provide a clear remediation message when the test fails (`Please fix the credentials and try again`)

Alternative: explain in the UI 'You'll need to visit /sync after this step to test the connection before sync activates.' Worse UX but minimal code change.
acceptance_criteria:
- For each non-Mintsoft integration in the wizard, call the test action after the save and surface the result
- Block the integration as 'complete' until the test passes (or until operator explicitly chooses to defer)
- Add a clear visual indicator (green check / red X with retry button)
- E2E test: complete WC onboarding → verify integration is fully enabled (sync flag true) without operator needing to visit /sync
notes: Filed during onboarding audit against recent PRs. The wizard's last touch was 7aa6d7fd (shopping connector product import) which predates PR #152's gate. This is a real UX regression introduced by the gate without an onboarding migration.
close_reason: Fixed: onboarding integration buttons and messaging now make save-and-test behavior explicit for WooCommerce/Shopify/Mintsoft and clarify that accounting OAuth Connect & Verify is required before the step can complete.

title: updatePreferredSuppliersForPlacedPurchaseOrder doesn't write activity log for the change — silent supplier reassignment (PR #156)
acceptance_criteria:
- Add per-product activity-log writes inside updatePreferredSuppliersForPlacedPurchaseOrder for the products whose preferredSupplierId actually changes
- Include previous supplier id/name, new supplier id/name, triggering PO id/reference in metadata
- Add a test asserting that an unchanged-supplier case (PO with the same supplier as already preferred) does NOT write an activity log entry
- Consider adding the activity entry to the auto-archive job too (already does this — verify symmetry)
notes: Filed during PR #156 review. Silent state changes are an audit anti-pattern (recurring theme in this repo's reviews — see PR #128, #129, #138 dedup silent paths).
