There is no first-class VAT report module in IMS. The only aggregation is groupVatBreakdown() inside app/actions/xero-daily-batch.ts:99-141, used to assemble the Xero daily batch payload. Tax liability reporting is effectively delegated to the accounting connector.

If a customer doesn't run Xero/QB or wants to reconcile before posting, they have no answer.

Fix direction: a VAT report page that takes a date range and outputs by tax-rate breakdown of net, tax, gross for both sales and purchases. Reuse the existing snapshot fields (taxRatePercent, taxBase, totalBase) so it stays consistent with what is sent to Xero.
close_reason: Already satisfied on development: native VAT reporting exists at app/(dashboard)/analytics/vat/page.tsx, backed by getVatReport in lib/domain/finance/finance-period-analytics.ts. It reports sales output VAT and purchase input VAT by tax rate/jurisdiction using persisted tax snapshot fields, with focused coverage in tests/domain/finance/finance-period-analytics.test.ts.

title: Multi-currency reporting: roll up by document currency, not just base
description:
Reports (sales-stats, dashboard, product-profitability) aggregate exclusively against `*Base` fields. There is no view that, for example, shows USD revenue separately from GBP revenue.

Limitation: hard to answer 'how much business did we do in EUR last quarter' or 'what is our USD AR balance' without exporting and pivoting manually.

Fix direction:
- Add document-currency dimension to existing report queries.
- Optionally a dedicated 'multi-currency summary' page breaking down revenue, COGS, AR, AP per currency with both foreign and base-equivalent columns.
close_reason: Already satisfied on development: multi-currency reporting exists at app/(dashboard)/analytics/currency-summary/page.tsx, backed by getCurrencySummaryReport in lib/domain/finance/finance-period-analytics.ts. It rolls up sales, purchases, AR, and AP by document currency with foreign and base-equivalent columns, covered by tests/domain/finance/finance-period-analytics.test.ts.

title: [bug] Decimal precision drift across 2/4/6dp boundaries on large batches
description:
The codebase uses three different precision conventions:
- 6dp for COGS (`unitCostBase`, Decimal(18,6))
- 4dp for base-currency line/order amounts (Math.round(x * 10000) / 10000)
- 6dp for Xero CurrencyRate exports
- 2dp for GBP final display

Conversions between these layers (foreign → base → Xero → display) can accumulate sub-cent drift on documents with many lines or on aggregations across periods. Not catastrophic individually, but reconciliation tolerance against Xero needs to be loose to absorb it.

Fix direction:
- Audit conversion call sites and standardise on a single rounding helper that takes precision as an argument.
- Add a reconciliation test that runs a synthetic 1000-line order through SO → invoice → Xero payload and asserts |IMS - Xero| < epsilon.
- Document the chosen rule and why each precision exists.

Cross-cutting; relates to onetwo3d-ims-hyz, -a8u, -ahu.
close_reason: Already satisfied on development: Decimal helpers provide roundMoney(currency minor units) and roundQuantity(precision) with ROUND_HALF_UP, docs/development.md documents the rounding policy and Math.round negative-midpoint divergence, tests/domain/math/decimal.test.ts covers 1000-line large-batch rounding boundaries, and tests/xero-fx.test.ts plus tests/qbo-fx.test.ts pin 6dp connector FX-rate payload rounding.

title: Verify Xero credit-note Type field: ACCREC vs ACCRECCREDIT
description:
Pre-existing concern surfaced in the Phase 1 Codex review of the FX work.

lib/connectors/xero/credit-notes.ts:58 currently sets:
  Type: 'ACCREC'

Per the Xero CreditNote OpenAPI schema, the Type enum is 'ACCRECCREDIT' / 'ACCPAYCREDIT' (NOT 'ACCREC' / 'ACCPAY' — those are Invoice types).

Credit notes have apparently been syncing in production, so either:
- Xero is lenient on this field for the CreditNotes endpoint, OR
- the credit note is being silently miscategorised (e.g. coerced to a default type)

Action:
1. Verify in a Xero sandbox what actually happens when 'ACCREC' is posted to /CreditNotes — does it succeed, fail, or coerce?
2. If wrong, change to 'ACCRECCREDIT' and add unit/integration coverage.
3. Check git history for why ACCREC was originally used (could be a deliberate workaround).

Not introduced by the FX work but the FX changes now ride on this code path, making it worth resolving.
close_reason: Already satisfied on development: lib/connectors/xero/credit-notes.ts uses ACCRECCREDIT and tests/xero-fx.test.ts pins the Xero CreditNotes sales-credit enum.

title: Manufacturing has no partial-completion / scrap recording
description:
qtyProduced is always set to qtyPlanned on completion. No field for actual_qty_completed or scrap_qty.

Refs:
- app/actions/manufacturing.ts:928

Fix direction: add actualQty + scrapQty fields, surface in completion form, recompute unit cost from actualQty.
close_reason: Closing as feature request, not a code-review bug. Adding actualQty/scrapQty fields and rebasing unit cost on actual output is a focused but real product change (schema migration + UI + tests). File as a manufacturing-roadmap task.

title: BOM lacks scrap/yield, substitutions, and nesting
description:
BomItem has only (parentProductId, componentProductId, qty). Missing capabilities:
- scrap percentage / yield loss
- alternative components (substitutions)
- nested BOMs (sub-assemblies)

Refs:
- prisma/schema.prisma — BomItem model
- app/actions/manufacturing.ts

Tradeoff vs scope: each is a meaningful schema + UI change. Defer until a real customer request, but capture as known limits.
close_reason: Closing as feature request, not a code-review bug. Nested BOMs, scrap/yield, and component substitution are real product gaps but each requires schema work (BomItem extensions or new tables), UI work, and product alignment. Not a 'fix' — file as a manufacturing-roadmap epic when the customer ask materialises.

title: Xero payment poll excludes WooCommerce orders
description:
Payment polling only reconciles manual orders (shoppingLinks.none). WC orders rely on webhooks for payment capture; missed/late webhooks leave WC orders stuck in PENDING_PAYMENT even when Xero shows them paid.

Refs:
- lib/connectors/xero/payment-poller.ts:29-35

Fix direction: include WC orders in the poll scope, gated by a 'reconcile via Xero' setting per connector instance.
close_reason: Closing as known design choice. The exclusion at payment-poller.ts:35 (shoppingLinks: { none: {} }) is intentional and documented in the file header comment ('Sales: manual orders only (WC orders arrive with paidAt already set)') — WC payment status is owned by WC webhooks. This is a separation of concerns, not a bug. A defensive fallback (poll Xero for WC orders unpaid > N days) would be a useful feature but needs product input and is out of scope for code-review fixes. File a feature request if the missed-webhook scenario becomes operationally important.

title: Activity log gaps: shipment creation, MFG fallback, sync skips
description:
Several mutating paths bypass logActivity() despite the project rule that every mutation logs. Backfill these calls.

Missing log calls:
- app/actions/allocation.ts:1089 — shipment creation (allocation confirm logs at :1135 but individual shipments don't)
- app/actions/manufacturing.ts:561-565 — disassembly cost-layer fallback uses console.warn
- lib/shopping.ts:89-250 — stock-sync skips
- (no bill-edit path exists today; tracked separately)
close_reason: Backfilled activity-log calls: shipment IDs in allocations_confirmed metadata; new disassembly_recovery_fallback WARNING log replacing console.warn in MFG; new stock_sync_skip_summary log aggregating skipped product reasons in lib/shopping.ts

title: Stock-sync silently filters out products with no telemetry
description:
buildShoppingStockUpdates filters out products by lifecycle status / missing warehouse map without logging. Sync reports success=true with 0 updates and the user has no way to know why.

Refs:
- lib/shopping.ts:89-250

Fix direction: return per-product skip reasons; log skipped count to activity log; surface in the sync run summary.
close_reason: Resolved as part of onetwo3d-ims-tlq: lib/shopping.ts now tracks per-reason skip counts in buildShoppingStockUpdates and emits a stock_sync_skip_summary activity log entry (level WARNING when nothing was pushed). Connector ID is included in metadata.

title: Xero rate-limit budget is in-memory only — resets on Node restart
description:
Minute/day buckets reset on every Node restart. Deployments mid-sync trigger cascading 429s on the new process.

Refs:
- lib/connectors/xero/api.ts:83

Fix direction: persist bucket counters to Redis or a small DB table keyed on tenant ID.
close_reason: Verified: minor concern, not a real bug in practice. In-memory rate-limit buckets reset on Node restart, BUT processPendingXeroSync caps at MAX_PER_RUN=50 (sync-processor.ts:19) and runs every 5 min — i.e. ~10 req/min average vs Xero's 60/min cap. Even with cold-start bursting, 50 req in <60s is at most 83% of the per-minute cap (one process). The exponential backoff on 429 (api.ts:83-88) handles the rare case where we collide with another process. Persisting buckets to Redis would be a defensive improvement but isn't a bug fix.

title: Xero invoices stuck in DRAFT after payment lands
description:
Posting mode (DRAFT vs AUTHORISED) is decided once at sync time. There is no follow-up to promote a DRAFT invoice to AUTHORISED when payment is detected.

Refs:
- lib/connectors/xero/sync-processor.ts:233-235 (comment acknowledges the distinction), :246-250

VERIFY: may not be IMS's responsibility if the Xero workflow handles AUTHORISED transitions itself.
close_reason: Closing as not-actionable. Posting mode is decided per AccountingSyncLog entry; downstream tooling (Xero web UI, Xero approver workflows) is responsible for promoting DRAFT to AUTHORISED. Building an IMS-side promotion job duplicates Xero's invoice approval workflow, which is the auditor-preferred path. If a customer has DRAFT invoices that need to be AUTHORISED en masse on payment, that's a Xero workflow rule, not an IMS code change.

title: [g5u2.5] freight PO detail UI: record/post credit note + block-reason
description: Freight PO detail page: when cancellation is blocked by a recorded invoice, show 'Record a supplier credit note first' with a modal to record (amount auto-filled from the freight invoice) and post the credit note; once POSTED, enable cancel. Blocked by g5u2.3, g5u2.4.
close_reason: PR #222 merged. SupplierCreditNotesCard on PO detail: list + record dialog + post; getPurchaseOrder returns supplierCreditNotes. Codex per-row-pending + a11y fixes applied.

title: [audit-M-mfg #4] Surface disassembly equal-split overhead fallback on the MO
description: Disassembly recovery falls back to equal-split overhead when recovered cost layers lack source lines (component-consumption.ts). It already logs a WARNING (disassemblyFallback). Finding asks to also surface it on the MO detail (a chip/note) so the operator sees the cost split was approximate. Low priority. Split from rcjf (PR #207).
close_reason: PR #221 merged. usedDisassemblyFallback persisted (on either provenance OR equal-split-overhead fallback, per Codex review) + amber chip on MO detail.

title: markSalesOrderPaid lacks FOR UPDATE — concurrent addPayment can double-warn paid_without_invoice
description: markSalesOrderPaid reads so via plain findUnique (no tx/FOR UPDATE) then updates. A concurrent addPayment that also sets paidAt can cause both paths to emit a paid_without_invoice WARNING for the same transition (log-only duplicate). Codex review F7 (H2, PR #202). Pre-existing lock gap; wrap the read+update in a FOR UPDATE tx.
close_reason: PR #211 merged. mmvp: markSalesOrderPaid now locks the row (FOR UPDATE) like addPayment. s3en: paid_order_cancelled_without_invoice warning at cancel of fully-paid uninvoiced orders. Partial-payment variant split to 45kd.

title: [audit-H1b] WooCommerce order-import shipping ignores reverse-charge tax type
description:
Codex review of PR #192 (H1) noted a pre-existing gap on a separate code path: lib/connectors/woocommerce/sync/order-import.ts sets the accounting payload shippingTaxType from accountingTaxType directly, never consulting TaxRate.reverseCharge. A reverse-charge WC order with a shipping line posts shipping under the standard code. Not introduced by H1 (which fixed the queueSalesInvoiceForOrder + credit-note paths) — WC import builds its own payload.

Fix direction: route the WC-import shipping tax type through the same lib/accounting/reverse-charge.ts:resolveSalesLineTaxType helper, OR (matching the H1 decision) leave shipping unswapped on both invoice and credit note and confirm WC import does the same. Verify the chosen rule is consistent across queueSalesInvoiceForOrder, the credit-note builder, and WC import.

Low priority unless reverse-charge WC orders with shipping are a real scenario. Acceptance: WC-import accounting payload shipping tax type matches the rule used by the native invoice push for the same reverse-charge order.
close_reason: WC-import goods lines now apply the reverse-charge tax-code swap (+ mapped-rate flag propagation found in review). PR #209 merged.

title: Mirror multi-component tax rate warning on purchase invoice creation/update
description:
PR #184 added a sales_invoice_tax_components_not_pushed WARNING in queueSalesInvoiceForOrder for orders whose lines use multi-component TaxRates. The purchase side (createInvoice + updateInvoice in app/actions/purchase-orders.ts) has the same architectural problem — purchase bills with multi-component TaxRates also post a single accountingTaxType, but no warning fires. Operators who never look at sales orders with these rates will never see the warning.

Fix direction: extract the multi-component detection from queueSalesInvoiceForOrder into a small helper (lib/accounting/multi-component-warning.ts) that takes the line tax-rate snapshots and returns the set of affected rate names. Call it from createInvoice + updateInvoice with the appropriate entity type (PURCHASE_ORDER) and action (purchase_invoice_tax_components_not_pushed). Same metadata shape as the sales-side warning.

A helper-level test covering both empty and multi-component inputs would also cover the sales-side path retroactively (closing PR #184's observation 5).
acceptance_criteria: Purchase invoices created or updated with at least one multi-component TaxRate emit a purchase_invoice_tax_components_not_pushed WARNING activity log with the same metadata shape as the sales-side warning. A helper test exercises both empty and populated input sets.
close_reason: Fixed: extracted multiComponentTaxRateNames helper to lib/accounting/multi-component-warning.ts (6 unit tests). queueSalesInvoiceForOrder refactored to use it; createInvoice + updateInvoice mirror the same purchase_invoice_tax_components_not_pushed WARNING when applicable. Also fixed a latent oversight from PR #183: updateInvoice's PO line select was missing reverseCharge:true, which would silently disable the reverse-charge swap on bill edits.

title: Add UI filter control for VAT report reporting category
description:
PR #181 added the vatReportingCategory filter on FinanceAnalyticsFilters and getVatReport applies it correctly, but the VAT analytics page has no UI control to set it. Users have to manually edit the URL to use the filter, and financeAnalyticsFiltersForUi doesn't surface the current value back to the page — so even with the filter applied via URL, the UI gives no indication.

Fix direction:
1. Add a <Select> on app/(dashboard)/analytics/vat/page.tsx populated from the four PR #180 categories (DOMESTIC / REVERSE_CHARGE / EC_SALES / OSS) plus an 'All categories' default.
2. Plumb vatReportingCategory through financeAnalyticsFiltersForUi (extend FinanceAnalyticsFilterValues with an optional vatReportingCategory).
3. Update the FinanceAnalyticsReportPage filter form to read the value and write it back into the URL.
4. Optionally make the dropdown options come from the DB (distinct TaxRate.reportingCategory values) so user-added categories appear automatically.
acceptance_criteria: VAT analytics page has a category dropdown; selecting an option reloads with vatReportingCategory in the URL; selecting 'All' clears it; the current selection is reflected back in the dropdown after navigation.
close_reason: Fixed in PR #182: VAT analytics page renders a reporting category select via showReportingCategory on FinanceAnalyticsReportPage; financeAnalyticsFiltersForUi forwards vatReportingCategory so the dropdown reflects URL state.

title: Refactor shared purchase invoice create/edit calculation path
description: PR #178 added updateInvoice alongside createInvoice, and the two paths now duplicate invoice line normalization, totals/tax calculation, accounting bill payload construction, and overbilling validation. This is acceptable for the feature PR but should be refactored into a shared purchase-invoice calculation/validation helper once the behavior has settled. While doing that refactor, also evaluate whether the per-line updates inside the edit transaction should move from sequential update calls to a bulk/concurrent pattern; current impact is minor because bill line counts are small, but the helper should make the write shape easier to reason about.
acceptance_criteria: createInvoice and updateInvoice share a purchase-invoice calculation/validation/payload helper with focused tests for product lines, cost lines, mixed VAT, overbilling limits, and idempotency payload stability. The transaction write shape for edited invoice lines is documented or optimized without changing accounting semantics.
close_reason: Fixed: createInvoice and updateInvoice now share purchase-invoice calculation, accounting payload, and line-limit validation helpers; tests cover mixed VAT lines, overbilling, idempotency payload stability, and update-sync queue branches.

title: Harden row-lock-queryraw guard regex to cover lowercase, $executeRawUnsafe, and composed-template forms
description:
PR #171 added tests/scripts/row-lock-queryraw.test.ts as a regression scaffold for SELECT ... FOR UPDATE rows that should use $queryRaw. The current regex /\$executeRaw(?:\([^`]*Prisma\.sql)?`SELECT[^`]*`/g + a case-insensitive FOR\s+UPDATE post-check has three known gaps:

1. The literal `SELECT` is uppercase-only — `await tx.$executeRaw\`select id from foo where id = ${x} for update\`` bypasses the guard.
2. `$executeRawUnsafe(...)` with a string literal containing `SELECT ... FOR UPDATE` isn't matched.
3. Composed templates like `const lock = Prisma.sql\`SELECT id FROM x WHERE id = ${id} FOR UPDATE\`; await tx.$executeRaw(lock)` are missed because the regex requires the template to be inline with the `$executeRaw` call.

None of these patterns exist in the codebase today (verified), so this is future-proofing only. Tighten the regex to make `SELECT` case-insensitive (use a flag rather than character class), add an `$executeRawUnsafe` branch with a SELECT/FOR UPDATE string-literal match, and add a heuristic for `Prisma.sql\`SELECT...FOR UPDATE\`` bound to a variable that's then passed to `$executeRaw(...)`.
acceptance_criteria: Guard test catches all three gap cases as offenders (verified by temporarily introducing one and confirming the test fails), while still passing on the current codebase.
close_reason: Fixed: hardened the row-lock guard to catch lowercase SELECT, executeRawUnsafe string calls, and Prisma.sql row-lock templates passed to executeRaw; the stricter guard also found and converted remaining multiline SELECT ... FOR UPDATE executeRaw call sites.

title: Add action-level cancelPurchaseOrder test covering requirePermission + revalidatePath
description:
PR #169 extracted cancelPurchaseOrder orchestration into lib/domain/purchasing/cancellation-service.ts and reduced the action to a 9-line wrapper. The service test imports and exercises cancelPurchaseOrderService directly with explicit deps, but it no longer asserts the revalidatePath calls that were on the original action test. Because revalidatePath now lives only in the action wrapper, a regression that removes the revalidatePath('/purchase-orders') and revalidatePath('/purchase-orders/' + id) calls — or that drops the requirePermission('purchasing.create') guard — would silently ship.

Add one tiny action-level test in tests/app/actions or tests/domain/purchasing that mocks cancelPurchaseOrderService and asserts: (a) cancelPurchaseOrder rejects when requirePermission throws, (b) on service.success the two revalidatePath calls happen, and (c) the result from the service is returned verbatim. The test keeps the domain layer untouched and only exercises the wrapper.
acceptance_criteria: An action-level test exists for cancelPurchaseOrder that mocks the service and asserts revalidatePath is called on success and requirePermission throws are surfaced. The test runs under tsx --test without needing a live database.
close_reason: Fixed: cancelPurchaseOrder action orchestration moved into a testable helper covering requirePermission, service result propagation, and success-only revalidatePath calls.

title: Document the lockMutableSalesOrders -> updateSalesTaxLines invariant in tax snapshot refresh
description: PR #164 batched the parent-total UPDATE with an 'AND orders.status IN (...)' WHERE filter. updateSalesTaxLines on the line table has no such filter. Today this is safe because lockMutableSalesOrders holds SELECT ... FOR UPDATE on the relevant sales_orders rows for the duration of the transaction, so no concurrent status change can land between line update and parent update. A future refactor that adds code between lockMutableSalesOrders and updateSalesTaxLines could break the invariant silently. Add a code comment above updateSalesTaxLines (and the purchase equivalent) noting the invariant so it's preserved across refactors.
acceptance_criteria: lib/tax/document-tax-snapshot-refresh.ts has a comment above updateSalesTaxLines (and updatePurchaseTaxLines) explaining that the lack of a parent-status filter is deliberate because of the FOR UPDATE row lock acquired by lockMutableSalesOrders / the equivalent purchase function.
close_reason: Fixed: documented that tax line updates rely on parent SELECT ... FOR UPDATE locks in the same transaction before omitting duplicate parent-status predicates.

title: Add auth-rejection path test for stock-position/inventory-ledger/inventory-costing CSV exports
description: PR #161 added new get*ExportResponse functions that resolve dependencies including requireApiAuth and accessDenied. The tests' authorizedRouteDeps mock both to authorized/null, so the route's auth-rejection short-circuit is not exercised. Add a test per route that makes the accessDenied mock return a NextResponse rejection, and asserts the route surfaces that response without invoking any report-loading dependency.
acceptance_criteria: Each touched route has a test that injects a denying accessDenied stub and asserts (a) the returned response is the stub-provided rejection, and (b) the report-loading deps were not called.
close_reason: Fixed: CSV export route tests now assert auth-denial responses short-circuit before report loading for stock-position, inventory-ledger, and inventory-costing exports.

title: dolt-share verification probe (delete me)
description: If you can read this from staging clone, the dolt store is genuinely shared.
close_reason: dolt-share verified

title: Add comment on module-load capture in defaultCancelPurchaseOrderDeps
description: PR #159 introduced defaultCancelPurchaseOrderDeps in app/actions/purchase-orders.ts as a top-level const binding production implementations. Because it captures function references at module-load time, a future test framework that swaps module-level functions would not see the swap reflected through cancelPurchaseOrder default-deps callers. The project's tsx + explicit-injection style sidesteps this, but the gotcha is non-obvious.
acceptance_criteria: defaultCancelPurchaseOrderDeps has a JSDoc-style comment explaining module-load capture and recommending explicit deps for test code.
close_reason: Fixed: defaultCancelPurchaseOrderDeps now documents module-load capture semantics and instructs tests to pass explicit deps when alternate behavior is needed.

title: Document idempotency behaviour when fxRateToBase changes between requeues
description:
Concern surfaced in the Phase 1 Codex review.

accountingPayloadKey() (app/actions/purchase-orders.ts:27) hashes the full payload as the idempotency key. With the new currencyRateToBase field included in the payload, a re-queue of the same document where the FX rate has changed produces a *different* hash, which means:

- queue-level dedupe at lib/accounting.ts:157-169 misses (different key)
- Xero idempotency at lib/connectors/xero/api.ts:110-112 is entry-id based, so it also won't dedupe

In theory this could allow a duplicate Xero post if the rate changed between the original queue and a retry. In practice rates don't change between retries (the rate was already stamped on the IMS document at creation time and we read it from there, not re-fetched), but the behaviour is non-obvious.

Action:
1. Document this in the unified-FX plan (docs/todo/unified-fx-rates-plan.md) under 'Open questions' or a new 'Idempotency' section.
2. Decide: should accountingPayloadKey() exclude currencyRateToBase from the hash so a rate change doesn't produce a new key? OR should it be included so a deliberate re-stamp does produce a new post?
3. Either way, add a code comment explaining the choice.

Low priority — this is a 'nice to have clarification', not a known bug.
close_reason: Already satisfied on development: docs/todo/unified-fx-rates-plan.md documents document-stamped FX idempotency behavior, and tests/xero-fx.test.ts asserts accounting payload keys include currencyRateToBase.

value: When filing bd issues as PR-review follow-ups, push the resulting .beads/issues.jsonl change to the SAME PR's branch (not to whatever branch the local working tree is on). Workflow: check out the PR's branch (gh pr checkout N), file bd issues, commit only .beads/issues.jsonl, push to the PR branch. This keeps review findings tied to the PR's history and avoids creating drive-by commits on unrelated feature branches.

Prisma P2028 'Transaction already closed: A rollback cannot be executed on a committed transaction' on a StockMovement createMany is almost always a RED HERRING. The real cause is a DEFERRABLE INITIALLY DEFERRED constraint trigger (stock_movements_reporting_evidence_guard, migration 20260602103000) firing at COMMIT — createMany's batch transaction mis-surfaces the deferred RAISE EXCEPTION as P2028. To see the true error, reproduce the insert with a single db.stockMovement.create() instead of createMany: it surfaces the actual message (e.g. 'Outbound stock movement requires matching COGS evidence'). The guard requires every outbound movement (SALE_DISPATCH/PRODUCTION_OUT/outbound ADJUSTMENT) to have a cogs_entries row; it broke the forecasting historical import (zero-cost, warehouse-less SALE_DISPATCH, referenceType WcHistorical/CsvHistorical/WcInitialImport). Fixed in migration 20260616120000 by exempting exactly that shape, mirrored in lib/domain/inventory/invariants.ts requiresCogsEntryEvidence AND the SQL invariant collector (3 enforcement points must stay in sync). The app db client is a PrismaPg driver-adapter singleton over pg.Pool — after()/fire-and-forget was NOT the cause.

IMS staging (https://ims-stage.onetwo3d.co.uk, 10.0.3.99:3000) is run by the systemd service 'ims-stage-dev.service' as 'npm run dev' from /root/ims/onetwo3d-ims-isolated, with EnvironmentFile=/root/ims/onetwo3d-ims-isolated/.env.local (this is the ONLY place SETTINGS_ENCRYPTION_KEY and the correct AUTH_SECRET live; the app's plain .env lacks SETTINGS_ENCRYPTION_KEY). CORRECT deploy: (1) git pull --ff-only (it tracks origin/development) && npx prisma generate (if schema changed); (2) systemctl restart ims-stage-dev.service; (3) verify: systemctl is-active ims-stage-dev.service + curl -s -o /dev/null -w '%{http_code}' http://127.0.0.1:3000/login == 200. Dev mode compiles from disk, so NO build step. DO NOT run scripts/deploy.sh on this box: it targets the WRONG dir (/root/ims/onetwo3d-ims) and runs 'npm start' without SETTINGS_ENCRYPTION_KEY/correct AUTH_SECRET, which grabs port 3000, blocks the systemd service's auto-restart, breaks reading encrypted settings (e.g. PO ordering throws 'SETTINGS_ENCRYPTION_KEY is required') and invalidates sessions (JWTSessionError 'no matching decryption secret' -> users must re-login). The main checkout /root/ims/onetwo3d-ims is NOT the served instance. Both .env files point at the same DB onetwo3d_ims_dev@localhost.

Both /root/ims/onetwo3d-ims (main) and /root/ims/onetwo3d-ims-isolated (staging) share ONE bd Dolt database. Staging clone's .beads/embeddeddolt is a symlink to /root/ims/onetwo3d-ims/.beads/embeddeddolt. This means: (1) bd create/update/close in either workspace is immediately visible from the other — no import roundtrip. (2) JSONL export in either workspace reflects the SAME state, so the PR #157 'wrong workspace re-export' trap no longer applies for these two checkouts. (3) PR review worktrees in /tmp/pr<N> still have their OWN .beads/embeddeddolt (from the PR branch's content) — they are deliberately separate. Setup commit: 5a4f57e ('chore(bd): make embeddeddolt gitignore match symlinks'). To rebuild from scratch after a fresh staging checkout: rm -rf /root/ims/onetwo3d-ims-isolated/.beads/embeddeddolt && ln -s /root/ims/onetwo3d-ims/.beads/embeddeddolt /root/ims/onetwo3d-ims-isolated/.beads/embeddeddolt

When filing bd follow-ups on a PR branch in a worktree, NEVER trust 'bd export' to give you a JSONL that respects the PR's pending closures — the worktree's bd uses its OWN .beads/embeddeddolt (from the branch), so bd commands in the worktree work against that workspace. The PR #157 trap (lost 49 closures) happened because I ran bd commands in the MAIN checkout, then copied its JSONL onto the worktree, overwriting the PR's closures. Safe workflows: (a) edit the PR branch's .beads/issues.jsonl DIRECTLY via Python/sed to append new issue rows — DO NOT call bd export; (b) work entirely in the worktree's bd (cd /tmp/pr<N> && bd create ...) so the export reflects PR state + your additions naturally; (c) the post-PR-#157 fix-up commit was fa5d214. See also dolt-store-shared-between-workspaces — that fix shares the dolt store across the main+staging checkouts but worktrees stay independent on purpose.
